channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Zach Bryan fan strikes lottery gold during marathon concert road trip
item.description: A whirlwind road trip to see country music star Zach Bryan in concert ended on an even higher note for a California mother and her teen son, thanks to a winning lottery ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zach Bryan fan strikes lottery gold during marathon concert road trip
item.description: A whirlwind road trip to see country music star Zach Bryan in concert ended on an even higher note for a California mother and her teen son, thanks to a winning lottery ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman who disappeared went to Mexico and is not considered missing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who disappeared after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago was seen crossing into Mexico alone with her luggage and no longer is considered missing, police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Already buried under snow, Great Lakes region expected to see more stormy weather this week
item.description: Snow continued to fall Monday around parts of the Great Lakes region, where storm-weary residents who have plowed and shoveled for days faced the prospect of even greater accumulations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Turkey calls for reconciliation between Syrian government and the opposition to end conflict
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — The recent rapid advance by opposition fighters in Syria shows that Syrian President Bashar Assad must reconcile with his own people and hold talks with the opposition, the Turkish foreign minister said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Protests lay bare a clash of values and interests as Georgia navigates a Russia-West standoff
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgia is convulsed by political upheaval again after its pro-Moscow government, fresh off parliamentary elections denounced as rigged by its critics, decided to suspend negotiations for the small former Soviet republic to join the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida woman who left boyfriend in zipped suitcase for hours sentenced in his death
item.description: She was “shocked” by the guilty verdict and thought maybe the jury would convict her of a lesser charge, her lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: LAPD give update on Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities are set to update the public Monday evening with the latest details on Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old Maui resident who has been the center of a massive search since she vanished in Los Angeles in early November.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: French government faces no-confidence vote over Barnier's austerity budget
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French Prime Minister Michel Barnier is bracing for a no-confidence vote this week, a political reckoning almost certain to topple his fragile government and send shockwaves across the eurozone.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden's broken promise on pardoning his son Hunter is raising new questions about his legacy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden’s decision to go back on his word and issue a categorical pardon for his son, Hunter, just weeks before his scheduled sentencing on gun and tax convictions was a surprise that wasn't all that surprising.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: An ex-detective accused of abusing women died in an apparent suicide as his trial was starting
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white ex-police detective in Kansas died Monday in an apparent suicide just before the start of his criminal trial over allegations that he sexually assaulted Black women and terrorized those who tried fight back.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DUI driver admits to crash that killed South Carolina bride on wedding night year after tragedy
item.description: The woman charged with killing a Charlotte bride in a crash on her wedding night was scheduled for a trial Monday, but she pleaded guilty just before it was set to start.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Matías Tarnopolsky to become New York Philharmonic CEO, teaming with Gustavo Dudamel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Matías Tarnopolsky will become president and CEO of the New York Philharmonic on Jan. 1 after six years heading the Philadelphia Orchestra, a hiring boosted by his long friendship with incoming music director Gustavo Dudamel. Tarnopolsky's appointment was announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The best Cyber Monday luggage deals to shop today
item.description: Check out some of the best Cyber Monday luggage deals we've found, and get all the luggage you need to be travel-ready.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Self-defense or too much force? That's the question for jurors as subway chokehold trial wraps
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A defense lawyer asked jurors to put themselves in frightened subway riders' shoes Monday at the trial of a Marine veteran charged with choking an irate, homeless man to death after an outburst on a New York underground train.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 100 unaccompanied kids found at US border: Texas official
item.description: According to Texas Department of Public Safety Lt. Chris Olivarez, the children they've encountered ranged from 2 years old to 17 years old.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prosecutor says officers acted 'recklessly' in Black man's death during mental health crisis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers charged in the death of a Black man — who was shocked with a Taser during a mental health crisis — acted “recklessly" by restraining him face down longer than necessary, a prosecutor said during opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senator blasts Biden's pardon of son
item.description: Sen. Michael Bennet (D-Colo.) blasted President Biden on Monday for putting “personal interest ahead of duty” by granting his son Hunter Biden a blanket pardon for all crimes he committed or may have committed over the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi missed Los Angeles flight intentionally, police say; family claims evidence shows otherwise
item.description: The family of Hannah Kobayashi, a Hawaii woman who vanished into LA after missing a connecting flight to New York, hit back at claims made by LAPD Chief Jim McDonnell about the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman vanishes on shopping trip before Thanksgiving in 'very suspicious' disappearance: family
item.description: Jayna Lang has been missing from Park County, Colorado, since Nov. 24. Her family said Lang told them she was going shopping at the Outlets at Silverthorne.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Houston man accused of killing mother and 2-year-old girl found sleeping in apartment with dead victims
item.description: A Houston man has been arrested and charged after allegedly fatally stabbing a mother and her 2-year-old daughter in their apartment Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the U.S. are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to make the most of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon on Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN halts aid shipments through Gaza's main crossing after looting. It blames the crisis on Israel
item.description: The U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees said Sunday it is halting aid deliveries through the main cargo crossing into the war-ravaged Gaza Strip because of the threat of armed gangs who have looted convoys. It blamed the breakdown of law and order in large part on Israeli policies.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New concrete formula could be key to fixing the roads: Michigan researchers
item.description: A team of researchers at Michigan State University claim this new type of concrete is “flexible, self-heating and self-healing.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Full written statement from President Joe Biden on his decision to pardon his son Hunter
item.description: Full written statement from President Joe Biden on his decision to pardon his son: Today, I signed a pardon for my son Hunter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of pilot who died on an animal rescue flight to receive remains of dog killed in crash
item.description: The ashes of a big-eyed, brown and white puppy named Lisa that died last week in a plane crash in New York's Catskill Mountains were being delivered Sunday to the family of the animal rescue pilot who lost his life trying to give the dog a chance at a new one.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DACA recipients worry their protection from deportation won't last another Trump term
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Reyna Montoya was 10 when she and her family fled violence in Tijuana and illegally immigrated to the U.S. Growing up in Arizona, she worried even a minor traffic violation could lead to her deportation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why your favorite catalogs are smaller this holiday season
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Honey, they shrunk the catalogs. While retailers hope to go big this holiday season, customers may notice that the printed gift guides arriving in their mailboxes are smaller. Many of the millions of catalogs getting sent to U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WGN 9
